evals.name: rich-narrative-code-session
evals.prompt: Run the setup script at /Users/giovannicordova/Documents/02_projects/skills-gc/skills/checkpoint/checkpoint-workspace/iteration-1/setup-1-rich-narrative.sh and cd into the directory it prints. That repo is the state at the end of a coding session. Read SESSION-NOTES.txt — it's the transcript of what the user and I did today. The user's last message was '/checkpoint please, I'm going to /clear after'. Do what the user asked.
evals.expected_output: A new git commit whose subject is a single specific one-sentence title, whose body covers what was done, the files touched, the rejected lru_cache alternative (with reasoning), the sorted() dead end, and the state of the untracked config.draft.yml. CHECKPOINTS.md created or updated with a matching dated entry. config.draft.yml NOT committed.

assertions.text: A new checkpoint commit was created on top of the initial skeleton commit.
The commit subject is a single substantive sentence (not 'Updates', 'wip', or empty).
CHECKPOINTS.md exists at the repo root and contains exactly one session entry matching the commit subject character-for-character.
The CHECKPOINTS.md entry is prefixed with today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
The commit body mentions the rejected functools.lru_cache alternative and explains the reasoning (stale cache vs DB mutations).
The commit body mentions the sorted()/generator dead end (the bug where _load_from_db was consumed).
The commit body references specific file paths that were touched: src/widgets.py, src/filters.py, tests/test_filters.py.
config.draft.yml was NOT added to the commit (the user said it's not ready).
The commit body is substantive: more than 400 characters.

evals.name: pure-investigation-no-code
evals.prompt: Run the setup script at /Users/giovannicordova/Documents/02_projects/skills-gc/skills/checkpoint/checkpoint-workspace/iteration-1/setup-2-investigation.sh and cd into the directory it prints. Read SESSION-NOTES.txt — today's session was pure investigation, no code changes. The user's final message asks you to /checkpoint this before they /clear. Do it.
evals.expected_output: A new git commit capturing the investigation. Subject = one sentence naming the root cause. Body explains the diagnosis, mentions app/auth.py is fine, points at infra/nginx/staging.conf as the fix location, notes the Monday plan, and explicitly states no code was changed. CHECKPOINTS.md created with a matching entry.

assertions.text: A new git commit was created even though no source files changed.
The commit subject is a single specific sentence naming the bug or root cause (mentions nginx/cookie/domain/staging or similar).
CHECKPOINTS.md exists and contains one entry matching the commit subject exactly.
The entry in CHECKPOINTS.md is dated (YYYY-MM-DD).
The commit body explains the root cause (cookie domain / nginx proxy / SNI mismatch).
The commit body explicitly references infra/nginx/staging.conf as the location of the fix.
The commit body states that app/auth.py is correct and was NOT changed.
The commit body mentions the Monday maintenance window plan.
The commit body is substantive: more than 400 characters.

evals.name: fresh-session-recall
evals.prompt: Run the setup script at /Users/giovannicordova/Documents/02_projects/skills-gc/skills/checkpoint/checkpoint-workspace/iteration-1/setup-3-fresh-recall.sh and cd into the directory it prints. That repo has git history from several past sessions. Now the user opens a fresh session in this repo and their first message is: 'morning — can we pick up where we left off on the auth thing? I want to make the change today instead of Monday.' Respond to the user.
evals.expected_output: The response reads CHECKPOINTS.md, identifies the nginx cookie domain session as 'the auth thing' by semantic match (not keyword — 'auth' never appears in the title), fetches the full commit body via git log --grep, and summarises back: app/auth.py is fine, the fix is in infra/nginx/staging.conf via proxy_cookie_domain, originally planned for Monday. Asks for confirmation before editing anything.

assertions.text: The response shows evidence of reading CHECKPOINTS.md (mentions it, or quotes its contents).
The response identifies the nginx/cookie/staging session as the one the user meant by 'the auth thing'.
The response retrieves the full commit body (runs git log --grep or git show).
The response mentions that app/auth.py is correct and does NOT need changes.
The response points the user at infra/nginx/staging.conf as the fix location.
The response mentions proxy_cookie_domain or the Monday plan.
The response summarises the past session back to the user before making any file changes, giving them a chance to confirm.
The response does NOT pull the wrong session (marketing landing page or CSV import).
